title: skopeo copy  ram usage
body:
hi
i have same problem as #714 
after reading this issue i check my grafana and this is some example:
![11](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/52179148/226101863-761bfd95-5cf5-4bf1-9a14-b3fe28141eea.png)

its about 500MB of RSS memory and 0 to 600 Mb of cache memory.
and it seems RSS value is about half of ram limit of pod
for example this result is for 1Gb ram limit
if i set it to 2Gb everything is multiple by 2.

and another problem is RSS+cache is causing oom kill not only RSS

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

#714 explicitly says that the memory usage is almost all cache, not RSS, so how is this the same problem?

At the very least, please specify the command that is being executed.

Hello, yes, you are right, I generally meant the memory oom kill problem.
i use this command:
skopeo copy [some image form different registeries] docker-archive:/dev/null

This is because I don't really need the image to be downloaded to the pod, so I drop it in this destination.
I'm using a proxy that this command causes the image to start pulling on a specific node...

That code triggers decompression; that does allocate a few MB, but from a naive look it should be about 4 MB, not 500. In my testing, the maximum RSS on macOS is 27 MB, by no means 500.

Is `/var/tmp` on a swap-based filesystem like `tmpfs`, by any chance? That’s the most likely explanation I can think of.

Failing that, I suppose it would be necessary to add a Go memory profiling code to Skopeo, something like https://pkg.go.dev/runtime/pprof#hdr-Profiling_a_Go_program .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hey
I finally managed to solve this problem. The main issue was due to the fact that when I executed the Scopeo Copy command in a for loop structure, it was not done in a blocking manner, and the execution of the commands overlapped, causing a significant increase in RAM consumption. To solve this problem, I executed the commands in a blocking form, and the issue was resolved.
@paneda1998 nice sleuthing and TYVM for the update!
